title: triticum sativum subsp. vulgare
intro: Triticum sativum subsp. vulgare, commonly known as common wheat, is a domesticated plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of common wheat include reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer, as well as providing essential nutrients like fiber, iron, and B vitamins. Common wheat can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
